description_long: This is a tall, freestanding metal scaffold structure with multiple horizontal platforms and cross-bracing for stability. The frame is constructed from tubular metal bars, with diagonal supports and four wide-angled legs for balance. There are two main platforms made of dark, likely wooden or composite planks, providing elevated surfaces for standing or placing items. A small vertical ladder is integrated into the lower section, allowing access to the upper platform. The open framework design allows visibility through the structure, and the overall build suggests it is intended for temporary use in construction or maintenance tasks. The surfaces are predominantly metallic with some darker, possibly rubber or plastic, footings at the base.
description: A tall, open-frame metal scaffold with two dark platforms and a built-in ladder, supported by angled legs and cross-bracing.
description_view_0: Front view showing the integrated ladder, both platforms, and the open frame structure with cross-bracing.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the diagonal cross-bracing and the depth of the platforms, with less visibility of the ladder.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the ladder and platforms, but with a clearer view of the back supports.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the cross-bracing and the structure's width, with platforms visible from the side.
